steps: Click 'OAuth Apps' in the left sidebar
Fill in the Application name, Homepage URL, and Authorization callback URL
On the app page, copy the 'Client ID'
Click 'Generate a new client secret' and copy the secret immediately — it is only shown once

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth app's Client ID from the GitHub Developer Settings

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth app's Client Secret — shown only once at creation time

notes: Free for all GitHub accounts — no paid plan required
Client secret is shown only once; store it securely before leaving the page
For organization-owned apps, go to your org's Settings > Developer settings > OAuth Apps
Scopes are requested at authorization time; only request the minimum scopes needed
GitHub also supports fine-grained Personal Access Tokens for simpler single-user integrations

test_endpoint.description: Get the authenticated user's profile to verify OAuth token is valid
